body.title: Node registry: timestamp ID + Redis cache + calving protocol
body.result: node_id.py + node_registry DDL + migrate_node_ids.py (31651 mem + 541 rl migrated) + cortex.store() wired + 538 tests pass. BOOK_xxx IDs still generated by book_learner — pass-through allowed; migrate those callers separately.
body.description:
Build node_registry infrastructure (D256).

INERTIA: cortex.store() = MEDIUM — discussed with Akien 2026-03-29.

SCOPE IN:
- NEW: wild_igor/igor/memory/node_id.py — timestamp ID generator + serial calving counter
- NEW: claudecode/migrate_node_ids.py — migrate all nodes in memories, reading_list, interpretive_edges, twm_observations, ring_memory to new IDs
- DDL: node_registry table (node_id, table_name, row_id, machine_id, created_at)
- EDIT: memory/cortex.py cortex.store() — uses new ID format + writes to node_registry
- NEW: Redis lookup layer — node_exists(id)/node_locate(id) check Redis then Postgres

SCOPE OUT (do not touch):
- wg_* tables — word graph IDs are internal, not cross-referenced by facia/traversal
- brainstem/ — HIGH inertia, not needed
- models.py — Memory dataclass id field stays str, no change
- main.py, anthropic.py — no changes this ticket
- Calving logic — separate ticket

FORENSIC LOGGING:
- node_id.py: every new ID generated → ~/.TheIgors/logs/node_registry.log (ts, id, table, machine)
- migrate_node_ids.py: every renamed row → ~/.TheIgors/logs/node_migration.log (old_id, new_id, table, source_date)
- cortex.store(): existing forensic_logger call extended with new_id field

TESTS:
- tests/test_node_id.py: ID format, uniqueness under burst (100 IDs same second), swarm suffix logic
- tests/test_node_migration.py: fixture with 5 fake memory rows, run migration, assert new ID format + node_registry rows created
- Redis cache: hit/miss/fallback test with mock Redis
